channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran Launches Missile Attack on Tel Aviv in Retaliation for Latest Killings
item.description: Iranian state media confirmed the killings of Ali Larijani, who had served as Iran’s de facto leader recently, and a militia commander late Tuesday. Airstrikes rocked the Lebanese capital early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Dozens Dead in Pakistani Airstrike on Kabul
item.description: The attack hit a drug rehabilitation facility, and Afghanistan vowed to retaliate, risking an escalation of the conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Safiullah Padshah and Elian Peltier
item.media:description: The airstrike hit a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul, Afghan officials said.

item.title: Inside the Supply Line Delivering American Guns to Mexican Cartels
item.description: A surge of weapons is flowing from the U.S. to Mexico. These firearms — sourced from gun shops, shows, websites and apps — are funneled across the border to fuel the country’s most violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas and Paul Ratje
item.category: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives

item.title: At Least 23 Killed in Nigeria as Insurgent Attacks Go On Despite U.S. Help
item.description: A teaching hospital and two markets in the city of Maiduguri were hit in what a military spokesman said were suicide bombings by Boko Haram.
item.dc:creator: Ismail Alfa and Saikou Jammeh
item.media:description: Members of the Nigerian Red Cross with a victim of the bombings in Maiduguri, Nigeria, at a hospital on Monday.

item.title: Trump Signals He May Intervene in Cuba Following Iran and Venezuela
item.description: Still at war with Iran and in control in Venezuela, President Trump is signaling that he is about to intervene in another country.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas
item.media:description: People gathering on a street during a blackout on Monday in Havana.

item.title: What to Know About Ali Larijani, Iran’s Top Security Official
item.description: One of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s closest confidants, Mr. Larijani emerged as the face of the government after Mr. Khamenei was killed.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top national security official, emerged as the de facto leader of the country after the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Sucked Into War, Gulf Countries Face the Limits of U.S. Security Guarantees
item.description: Barraged by Iranian attacks and questioning the value of security ties with the United States, nations in the Gulf have turned to Ukraine, Australia and Italy for help.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Smoke billowing near the Dubai International Airport on Monday.

item.title: As American Bombers Take Off From R.A.F. Fairford in UK, Iraq War Looms Large
item.description: R.A.F. Fairford was the site of repeated antiwar protests during the Iraq war in 2003. Now it is being used again as a base for U.S. bombing missions in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Royal Air Force Fairford (Gloucestershire, England, Air Base)
item.media:description: U.S. Air Force personnel loading munitions into a B-1 bomber at R.A.F. Fairford on Monday.

item.title: What the Iran War Means for the U.S.-China Relationship
item.description: With the Xi-Trump summit almost certainly delayed, and tensions rising over the war in Iran, vital issues for both the U.S. and China are also being cast into uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Chinese destroyer Tangshan at dock near an Iranian navy ship in Cape Town, South Africa, in January for joint maritime safety operations.

item.title: As Carney Seeks Canadian Alliances with China, India and Qatar, He Looks Away on Human Rights
item.description: Canada’s prime minister chooses pragmatism in a turbulent world, which means doing business with countries that do not share Canada’s democratic values. Some critics see this as weakness.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada meeting Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India this month in New Delhi.

item.title: Peter Thiel Fears the Antichrist Is Coming. In Rome, Some Call His View Heresy.
item.description: The right-wing tech investor is giving lectures near the Catholic church’s administrative heart. Commentators there are rejecting his apocalyptic vision.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:credit: Nordin Catic/Getty Images for The Cambridge Union
item.media:description: Peter Thiel speaking in Cambridge, England, in 2024. He previously delivered lectures on the Antichrist in Paris in January and in San Francisco last year.

item.title: Tribunal Calls for Retrial of the Vatican’s ‘Trial of the Century’
item.description: Three years ago, the Vatican’s criminal court convicted a cardinal for the first time in history. Now, an appeals court has ruled there were irregularities in his prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:description: Cardinal Giovanni Angelo Becciu at the Vatican in 2024.

item.title: Europe Has Helped the U.S. Protect Shipping. The Strait of Hormuz Is Different.
item.description: European Union ministers and leaders pushed back on ideas to protect the strait, including expanding a maritime operation already in place in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A briefing about Operation Aspides in Greece in April 2024.

item.title: Intrigue, Power Plays and Rivalries: Inside the Rise of Mojtaba Khamenei
item.description: The weeklong fight over Iran’s next leader pitted the Revolutionary Guards against moderates. The generals won, but only over spirited resistance.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A photo of the new supreme leader of Iran, Mojtaba Khamenei, at a ceremony for military officials killed by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes, in Tehran, Iran, last week.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado, Ephrat Livni and Farnaz Fassihi

item.title: The Faroe Islands Are Changing Some of Europe’s Strictest Abortion Rules
item.description: A new law allowing abortion up to 12 weeks will be a major shift in an archipelago of 55,000 people, and there are strong feelings on both sides.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Regin Winther Poulsen
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Marna Jacobsen, 71, in Torshavn, Faroe Islands, late last year. Ms. Jacobsen said that as a 17-year-old she had to lie to get an abortion.

item.title: How TrumpRx Drug Prices Compare With Those in Other Countries
item.description: The TrumpRx website claims to offer the best prices for medications. Here’s where Americans still pay more — and much more.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins
item.media:description: President Trump unveiled the TrumpRx website last month. Visitors to the site are greeted by a promise: “Find the world’s lowest prices on prescription drugs.”

item.title: Trump’s Friendship With Japan’s Leader Takaichi Faces Test Over Iran
item.description: In Washington on Thursday, President Trump is expected to press Sanae Takaichi for military help in the Strait of Hormuz. But she faces constraints on what she can offer.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan aboard the U.S.S. George Washington at Yokosuka Port south of Yokohama, Japan, in October.

item.title: Netanyahu Hopes Strikes on Iran Will Lead to Uprising and Regime Change
item.description: Israeli attacks have targeted the command centers of Iran’s repressive, internal security forces in hopes that Iranians will overthrow their rulers. Some see that as wishful thinking.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Ronen Bergman and David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran shows Iran’s three supreme leaders.

item.title: As War Chokes Natural Gas Supply, Asia Turns Back to Coal
item.description: Across Asia, a sharp drop in liquefied natural gas supplies is pushing major importers back toward coal, undermining L.N.G.’s long-held role as a stable energy anchor.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: Qatar Energy announced a shutdown of its liquefied natural gas production after Iranian attacks on energy installations.

item.title: This is what happened on March 17.
Cuban Americans Will Be Allowed to Own Businesses in Cuba, but Is That Enough to Rescue the Economy?
item.description: The Cuban government announced that Cubans living abroad can own and invest in businesses, but experts said they were disappointed that the measures didn’t go further.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.media:description: People stand outside a store in Havana on Monday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: The deaths of two top Iranian officials were announced on Tuesday, while President Trump once again lashed out at NATO allies who refused to aid the U.S. war effort against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, center, registering his name as a candidate for presidential elections in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Trump’s Next Decision in War: Whether to Retrieve Iran’s Nuclear Fuel
item.description: A mission to seize or destroy Iran’s nuclear material would be one of the riskiest military operations in modern American history.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vantor, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A satellite image of the Pickaxe Mountain tunnel complex near the Natanz nuclear facility in central Iran last week. It is not certain where all of Iran’s nuclear material resides.

item.title: Kharg Island Is an Appealing Target for Trump, With High Risks
item.description: A U.S. attack or a move to seize control of Iran’s main oil export hub could cripple the country’s ability to profit from its natural resources. But it would also risk sending energy prices even higher.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Rebecca F. Elliott and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Kharg Island oil facility off the coast of Iran in 2017.

item.title: Ali Larijani, a Top Iranian Politician and Emissary, Is Dead at 67
item.description: Killed in an Israeli attack, he was Iran’s leading national security official and a confidant of the former supreme leader. He led a brutal crackdown on protests this year.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani in Tehran in 2024. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei made Mr. Larijani the de facto leader of Iran in January, when nationwide protests engulfed the country.

item.title: An Unexploded Bomb Near the Colombia-Ecuador Border Leads to a Diplomatic Clash
item.description: The Times photographed an unexploded munition in southern Colombia, near the Ecuadorean border. A high-stakes feud between both countries quickly ensued.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, John Ismay and Federico Rios
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: The remnants of a 500‑pound undetonated bomb dropped by the Ecuadorian army that fell on a coca crop in Colombia.

item.title: Is Latin America Abandoning Cuba?
item.description: President Trump is turning up the pressure on Cuba, but other Latin American countries, even those on the left, are staying quiet. We look at why.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Ali Larijani, Iran’s De Facto Political Leader, Killed by Israel
item.description: The killing, which Iran confirmed, could embolden Iranian hard-liners, as the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign continues.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Ronen Bergman and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top security official, in Tehran in 2024. He was killed in an overnight strike, the Israeli military said.

item.title: As Trump belittled European allies, the Irish leader pushed back, gently.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump hosted Micheál Martin, the taoiseach or prime minister of Ireland, at the White House on Tuesday,

item.title: Israel Keeps Killing Key Iranian Leaders. Will It Work?
item.description: Israeli officials trumpeted airstrikes that killed Iran’s de facto leader and the commanders of a militia notorious for violently suppressing protests. But “decapitation has its limits,” an analyst warns.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top security official, in Tehran in 2024. The Israeli military announced it had killed him in an airstrike on Tuesday.

item.title: The Iran War Is Pushing Some Countries to Cut Back On Energy Use
item.description: As the conflict enters its third week, some nations are trying to reduce energy use, including a mandatory energy holiday in Sri Lanka.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A damaged oil facility in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, on Saturday.

item.title: U.N. Says Attacks on Iran Set to Drive Hunger to Record Levels
item.description: Tens of millions more people around the world could face acute hunger as the war in the Middle East continues to disrupt shipping and drive up oil prices.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Volunteers prepared packages of food in the Paktia Province of Afghanistan on Monday.

item.title: China Ramps Up Scrutiny of Meta’s Acquisition of Manus
item.description: The country appears to be cracking down on people linked to the acquisition of Manus, a Singapore company with Chinese roots, as President Trump prepares to visit Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson, Meaghan Tobin, Paul Mozur and Eli Tan
item.media:description: Meta announced in December that it had bought Manus, a Singapore artificial intelligence start-up that has Chinese roots.

item.title: Antisemitic Attacks Bring Deeper Unease to Dutch Jews
item.description: Since a school and a synagogue were attacked over two days, an unsettling feeling has spread among Jews in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:description: Police standing outside a Jewish school following an explosion in Amsterdam on Saturday.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Doesn’t Need Allies to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has faced mounting criticism over the U.S.-Israeli assault on Iran from allies and even members of his own administration.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump spoke to reporters on Tuesday while meeting with Micheál Martin, the prime minister of Ireland, in the Oval Office.

item.title: A Smelly Ice Cream Ad Tempts Few London Commuters
item.description: Magnum Ice Cream is scaling back a scented ad at King’s Cross station after commuters complained that the aroma was “sickly” and “artificial.”
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.media:description: Every few seconds, spritzes of chocolate aroma fill King’s Cross Station in London, part of a marketing campaign for Magnum Ice Cream.

item.title: Ali Larijani Killing Fuels Anxiety in Iran
item.description: The death of Iran’s top national security official has ignited concern about the country’s future.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ali Larijani was appointed as the head of the Supreme National Security Council by the slain former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: An ‘Unprecedented’ Outbreak of Meningitis Raises Alarm in Britain
item.description: Two people have died in an outbreak of meningococcal disease. Many cases were traced to a nightclub in Canterbury that is popular with students at the University of Kent.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: Staff and students waiting in line to receive antibiotics at the University of Kent in Canterbury on Monday.

item.title: Under Roman Abramovich, Chelsea Broke Rules for Years, Premier League Says
item.description: The Russian oligarch spent nearly two decades building the London soccer club into a sporting superpower. That success was built partly on regulation breaches, an investigation found.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Mr. Abramovich celebrating Chelsea’s Champions League victory in Porto, Portugal, in 2021.

item.title: Why Won’t Europe Help Trump in Iran? Let’s Count the Reasons.
item.description: President Trump says his NATO allies are disloyal for not helping the United States to clear an oil blockade. But they have a long list of incentives to refuse.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An apartment in Tehran on Sunday after an airstrike.

item.title: What is the Basij?
item.description: The large, plainclothes militia group is deeply embedded in Iranian society and has been used to crush dissent, often with brutal tactics.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gholamreza Soleimani, commander of Iran’s Basij militia force, in Tehran, in 2019.

item.title: Joe Kent, a Top Counterterrorism Official for the Trump Administration, Resigns, Citing Iran War
item.description: Mr. Kent is the highest-ranking Trump administration official to quit in opposition to the conflict. He is also known for his support of conspiracy theories.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Robert Draper and Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: “I cannot in good conscience support the ongoing war in Iran,” Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, wrote in a letter to President Trump that he posted Tuesday.

item.title: Who Runs Iran Now? Charting the Top Leaders Killed in the War So Far.
item.description: Israel said on Tuesday that it had killed two more high-ranking figures, including the man who had essentially been running Iran for weeks.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Israel’s Killing of Ali Larijani Could Allow Military to Tighten Grip on Iran
item.description: As Iran’s top national security official, Mr. Larijani had a reputation for acting as a bridge between hard-line figures in the armed forces and more moderate political factions.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, head of the Supreme National Security Council of Iran, in Tehran in 2024. He oversaw the brutal crackdown on antigovernment protesters in January.

item.title: Another Tanker Is Attacked Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The ship was struck near an Emirati port, in the first such attack in five days. The United Arab Emirates also said it was intercepting Iranian drones and missiles.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A tanker anchored near Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates was hit by a projectile on Tuesday, a British monitoring agency said.

item.title: China is Tight-Lipped on Trump’s Request to Delay His Xi Summit
item.description: After President Trump said he needed to postpone his meeting in Beijing with Xi Jinping, a spokesman for China said officials were still discussing the timing of a meeting.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader, last met in Busan, South Korea, in 2025.

item.title: We Traced How U.S. Guns Get to Mexican Cartels
item.description: Gun traffickers have quietly moved what they say is an unprecedented number of weapons from the United States to Mexico. Our reporter Paulina Villegas followed the trail of these guns.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas, Rebecca Suner, Jon Miller, Gabriel Blanco, Joey Sendaydiego, Stephanie Swart, Paul Ratje for The New York Times and Guillermo Alvarez

item.title: This is what happened on March 16.
Gas Prices Keep Rising as Seasonal Changes Add to Oil Price Pressure
item.description: Shipping routes crucial to global commerce remained disrupted as the conflict in the Middle East continued.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Ronen Bergman, Farnaz Fassihi and David E. Sanger
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Monday
item.description: President Trump said he might postpone a trip to China to focus on the war. More than a million people have been displaced in Lebanon this month, according to the country’s health ministry.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An encampment in a parking lot in Beirut, Lebanon. Israel’s defense minister, Israel Katz, said that its forces had launched a “ground maneuver” in southern Lebanon, compounding fears of a broader invasion.

item.title: Trump Administration Seeks to Remove Cuba’s President From Power During Negotiations
item.description: The United States has told Cuba that for meaningful progress to be made in negotiations, President Miguel Díaz-Canel must step down, said people familiar with the talks.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles, Edward Wong and Annie Correal
item.media:description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel of Cuba during a visit to Venezuela last year.

item.title: A Predictable Problem
item.description: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a well-known problem, appears to have caught Trump off guard.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Hits Out at Allies After Cool Response to Requests for Warships
item.description: Australia, Germany and Japan said they weren’t planning to participate in efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump said his call for help was really a loyalty test.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Erica L. Green, David E. Sanger and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mr. Trump’s appeal for an international naval escort, which he issued on Saturday, was the first time he appeared eager to build a broad coalition against Iran.

item.title: Trump Officials Look to More Managed Approach to Trade With China
item.description: The administration has begun discussing a new “Board of Trade” with China to try to balance what U.S. officials see as an unequal economic relationship.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: Scott Bessent, the Treasury secretary, left, and Jamieson Greer, the U.S. trade representative, conducted talks with their Chinese counterparts on Sunday and Monday in Paris.

item.title: Trump Says He Will Have the ‘Honor’ of ‘Taking Cuba’
item.description: President Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal, Jack Nicas and Frances Robles
item.media:description: The Güinera neighborhood of Havana on Friday.

item.title: What Are Trump’s Positions on Iran? They Can Change by the Sentence.
item.description: The president is no stranger to staking out contradictory stands, part of what his aides say is his negotiating style. But on Iran, his shifting positions are colliding with the consequences of war.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump leaving the White House last month. The conflict in Iran has come with consequences including a rising death toll and soaring gas prices.

item.title: How the Makers of ‘Mr. Nobody Against Putin’ Addressed ‘an Ethical Minefield’
item.description: The documentary about Vladimir V. Putin’s wartime indoctrination of Russian schoolchildren won an Oscar at Sunday’s Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Paul Sonne
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: David Borenstein, in white, and Pavel Talankin, left, at the Oscars on Sunday, accepting an award for “Mr. Nobody Against Putin.”

item.title: In Choosing ‘Epic Fury,’ Trump Names a War and Defines His Presidency
item.description: The branding of the U.S. military operation against Iran is a quintessentially Trumpian choice for a leader whose tenure has been marked by anger.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
